PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Oct-30-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Mark Rodiles-Peterson for George W. Pratt, Petitioner.

The Court noted there are no objections.

The Court granted the Petition for Probate of Lost Will and for Letter Testamentary (subject to review of the order). Bond in the amount of $20,000.00 is required. Status on Close of Estate set for April 21, 2021 at 9:00am in Dept 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Shirley Le. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Oct-11-2019 Continued To Oct-30-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Sep-30-2019 Continued To Oct-16-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Sep-04-2019 Continued To Oct-02-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Mark Rodiles-Peterson for George Pratt (Petitioner).

The Court noted that the issue is overcoming presumption of Probate Code 6124, that if the Decedent wasn't competent at the time of death, it does not have to overcome the presumption of revocation and stated that Counsel may want to speak with the Decedent's Doctors as to his capacity. Mr. Rodiles-Peterson spoke of the Decedent's competency, stated that the will was not in George's possession, that George provided assistance to the Decedent, that the Decedent was involved in elder abuse and had things stolen from him, and that the thought is that the will got stolen based on other possessions that were in the safe deposit box. Counsel also stated that only the codicil was found and that Co-Counsel did not hear from the Decedent of any intent to revoke. The Court informed Counsel that it needs a statement from his Client as to why he thinks the will was stolen from the safe deposit box, where he looked for the will and a statement from his Co-Counsel as to the relationship he had with the Mr. Pratt and whether he retains original documents. The Petition for Probate of Lost Will and for Letters Testamentary is continued to October 2, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Aug-14-2019 Continued To Sep-04-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
